directive: Implement retry logic at the per-alert level, matching the existing per-alert dispatch shape. On 5xx or 429: exponential backoff, 3 attempts. On 4xx (except 429): fail-fast, no retry. Dedup_key on each payload prevents duplicate incidents if a retry fires after a silent partial success. Do not introduce batch-level retry state — the live dispatch path is not batched and p4 (backfill) already handles its own retry shape independently.
rationale: The p2 decision (per-alert mapper) does not formally constrain p5 — these are different layers — but the same reasoning applies independently. The live dispatch path is per-alert (existing dispatcher, p2 mapper); retry logic at the transport layer should match. Per-alert retry means only the failing alert retries, not all 99 others that may have succeeded in a co-batch. Bulk-batch retry forces re-sending already-accepted events on any partial failure, increasing unnecessary API traffic and accelerating rate-limit consumption — the opposite of the goal. Dedup_key satisfies the duplicate-prevention acceptance criterion either way, but per-alert retry trivially satisfies 'preserve alert ordering where possible' without any partial-batch state tracking. Bulk batching is a backfill concern (p4, already done); the live dispatch path does not batch.
